untranslatable_reason: High and Tight Flag pattern requires detection of specific chart pattern formation (flag structure with trend-line boundary). This requires pattern recognition beyond point-in-time indicator values. The 'trend-line boundary' cannot be precisely defined using the allowed indicators without access to pattern detection algorithms or historical pivot/support-resistance line calculations not available in the standard indicator set. Close above 'pivot' is used as a proxy breakout signal, but this is NOT equivalent to the HTF trend-line breakout described.
translation_notes: Signal relies on visual pattern recognition (HTF formation and trend-line boundary) which cannot be adequately translated into mathematical indicator conditions. The entry condition uses pivot as a proxy for a breakout level, but this is a significant simplification. The source text indicates the author uses 'close above HTF's trend-line boundary' as the primary entry signal, which requires explicit trend-line calculation from pattern highs/lows—not available in standard indicators. Success rates (64% bull, 0% failure at 5% threshold) suggest high-confidence pattern but cannot be validated without proper pattern detection.
